rule.description: TANGEDCO ICS anomaly detection event received

CRITICAL: SCADA historian data dropout detected. Loss of monitoring visibility. Possible ransomware-induced SCADA shutdown or wiper malware (TRITON/Industroyer-class). ATT&CK ICS: T0813 – Denial of View | T0816 – Device Restart/Shutdown. ISO 27001: A.8.16 Monitoring Activities.

HIGH: Sensor flatline or suspicious reading stability detected. Readings frozen at static value — physically impossible in live grid. Possible Man-in-the-Middle on IEC 104/DNP3 protocol (T0856 Spoof Reporting Message). ISO 27001: A.8.24 Cryptography (unauthenticated ICS protocols).

CRITICAL: Multiple sensor spoofing events from same OT zone within 1 hour. Escalation: sustained MITM attack on ICS protocol layer likely in progress. Immediate OT network isolation of affected zone required.

CRITICAL: Anomalous grid demand change detected — pattern consistent with unauthorised IEC 104 or DNP3 command execution. Sudden demand drop followed by surge — possible breaker open/close sequence. ATT&CK ICS: T0855 – Unauthorised Command Message. ISO 27001: A.8.20 Network Security.

HIGH: Grid frequency outside safe operating range (49.6–50.4 Hz). Could indicate simultaneous breaker operations caused by adversary commands, or natural fault — requires immediate correlation with SCADA command log. ATT&CK ICS: T0855 / T0826 – Loss of Availability.

CRITICAL CORRELATION: Demand manipulation AND frequency excursion within 5 minutes. High-confidence indicator of active SCADA attack in progress. Escalate to Tier 2 immediately. Consider switching affected substations to manual control. Notify SRLDC (Southern Regional Load Despatch Centre).

CRITICAL: Ransomware-induced SCADA blackout detected. IT systems compromised — OT monitoring has gone dark. Activate Incident Response Playbook: IRP-001 (Ransomware + IT/OT Pivot). ATT&CK ICS: T0816 Device Restart/Shutdown | T0882 Theft of Operational Data. ISO 27001: A.8.7 Malware Protection | A.8.13 Information Backup.

CRITICAL: Ransomware file extension detected on monitored host. Possible ransomware encryption in progress on SCADA historian or engineering workstation. Isolate host immediately. ATT&CK: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact.

rule.match: vssadmin delete shadows|wmic shadowcopy delete|bcdedit /set recoveryenabled no
rule.description: CRITICAL: Shadow copy deletion detected — classic ransomware pre-encryption step. Host: $(hostname). Isolate immediately and preserve forensic image before remediation. ATT&CK: T1490 Inhibit System Recovery.

HIGH: Network logon to OT/SCADA system from non-OT source workstation. Possible lateral movement from IT network to OT zone (IT-05 risk — flat network). Source: $(data.win.eventdata.workstationName) → OT target. ATT&CK ICS: T0886 Remote Services | T0812 Default Credentials.

HIGH: Anomalous grid demand change detected during off-peak hours (01:00–05:00). Change magnitude exceeds natural load variation threshold for this time window. Possible unauthorised SCADA operator access or stolen credential misuse. ATT&CK ICS: T0859 – Valid Accounts (Privileged). ISO 27001: A.8.5 Privileged Access Management.

MEDIUM: Successful authentication to OT/SCADA system outside business hours. User: $(data.srcuser) | System: $(data.dstuser) | Time: $(timestamp). Verify against approved change management schedule. ATT&CK ICS: T0859 Valid Accounts. ISO 27001: A.5.15 Access Control.

MEDIUM: SCADA/EMS system access on weekend detected. Weekend access requires approved change management ticket. Verify: $(data.srcuser) accessed $(data.dstuser) on $(timestamp). ISO 27001: A.5.15 Access Control | A.8.5 PAM.

HIGH: Same user account accessing SCADA systems multiple times outside business hours. User: $(data.srcuser). Pattern suggests credential misuse or active intrusion. Escalate to Tier 2. Consider temporary account suspension pending investigation. ATT&CK ICS: T0859 Valid Accounts.

CRITICAL: External internet traffic permitted to ICS protocol port. Port $(data.dstport) = ICS protocol (IEC 104/DNP3/Modbus/BACnet/OPC-UA/S7). Source: $(data.srcip). This should NEVER be allowed. Immediate investigation required — possible internet-exposed HMI. ATT&CK ICS: T0866 Exploitation of Remote Services.

HIGH: IEC 104/DNP3/Modbus traffic from non-authorised source IP. Source $(data.srcip) is not in approved SCADA master station subnet. Possible rogue master or lateral movement. ATT&CK ICS: T0855 Unauthorised Command | T0848 Rogue Master.

MEDIUM: Database port access from corporate LAN to OT/historian subnet. Source: $(data.srcip) → Target: $(data.dstip):$(data.dstport). Historian should be in DMZ (Z-2), not directly accessible from enterprise zone (Z-1). Remediation: Implement Purdue Model DMZ. ISO 27001: A.8.20 Network Security.

HIGH: Shared/service account login to OT system detected. Account: $(data.srcuser). Shared accounts violate ISO 27001 A.8.5 (PAM) and make attribution impossible for forensic investigation. Action: Identify actual user. Enforce individual named accounts.

CRITICAL: LSASS memory access from non-approved process on OT workstation. Source process: $(data.win.eventdata.sourceImage). Possible credential harvesting (Mimikatz) — precursor to lateral movement to SCADA systems. Isolate workstation immediately. ATT&CK: T1003.001 LSASS Memory.

HIGH: New user account created on OT/SCADA system. New account: $(data.win.eventdata.targetUserName). Created by: $(data.win.eventdata.subjectUserName). Verify against approved change ticket. Possible persistence mechanism. ATT&CK ICS: T0859 Valid Accounts. ISO 27001: A.5.18 Access Rights.

CRITICAL: Known high-severity CVE exploitation attempt detected. CVE: $(match). These CVEs are actively exploited in ICS/OT environments. Check patch status of affected systems immediately. ISO 27001: A.8.8 Management of Technical Vulnerabilities.

MEDIUM: Legacy ICS device or outdated firmware version detected. Device may have unpatched vulnerabilities. Schedule maintenance window for update. ISO 27001: A.8.8 Vulnerability Management | A.8.9 Configuration Management.

CRITICAL — ACTIVE ICS ATTACK CAMPAIGN DETECTED: Multiple attack-chain indicators observed within 2-hour window: - Off-hours privileged access (T0859) - IT-to-OT lateral movement (T0886) - Anomalous SCADA demand manipulation (T0855) This pattern matches Industroyer2 / Sandworm TTPs. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1. Notify CISO and OT Security Lead 2. Activate IRP-002 (SCADA Breach Playbook) 3. Alert SRLDC and CERT-In ICS team 4. Switch affected substations to manual control

CRITICAL — RANSOMWARE + SCADA IMPACT CONFIRMED: Ransomware encryption, shadow copy deletion, AND SCADA blackout detected within 1-hour window. This is an active ransomware incident with confirmed OT impact — not a drill. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1. Activate IRP-001 (Ransomware + IT/OT Pivot Playbook) 2. Isolate affected IT hosts via VLAN quarantine 3. Verify OT systems are on independent power / manual ops 4. Notify CERT-In within 6 hours (mandatory per CERT-In Directions 2022) 5. Preserve forensic images BEFORE remediation
